evals.prompt: I've been working on the snapping feature in SFGSurface. /context memorize snapping
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive .md memo file covering the snapping feature's architecture, key files, implementation details, and integration points

assertions.text: Memo includes relative file paths from project root to the snapping feature files
Memo describes the architecture/data flow (e.g., gesture -> calculator -> strategy -> result pipeline)
Memo lists and describes key source files with their roles
Memo captures non-obvious implementation details (e.g., center snap multiplier=2, fixed edge handling)
Memo mentions related files outside Snapping/ directory (Movable.swift, Resizable.swift, Scalable.swift)

evals.prompt: I need to continue working on snapping. /context restore snapping
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing memo, presents a brief summary, and is ready to work on the feature without re-exploring the codebase

assertions.text: Did NOT re-read source files — only read the memo file
Presents a brief summary of the loaded feature context

evals.expected_output: Tells the user no memo exists for 'payments' and suggests running /context memorize payments first

assertions.text: Reports that no memo/context exists for 'payments'
Suggests running memorize first (e.g., /context memorize payments)
Does NOT go exploring the codebase looking for payments-related code
